skill.name: oss-hunter
skill.description: Find promising open source issues by searching active GitHub repositories and preparing contribution dossiers.
skill.summary: Finds high-value GitHub issues and summarizes contribution strategy.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and network findings are real but expected for a GitHub issue discovery skill. The subprocess use invokes the GitHub CLI without a shell, which lowers injection risk, but publication should disclose local CLI and network requirements. Static weak-cryptography and most markdown backtick alerts are false positives based on the cited lines.

medium_findings.title: GitHub CLI Subprocess Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The helper script runs subprocess.run with the gh executable. It uses an argument list and does not invoke a shell, so direct shell injection evidence is low, but it still executes a local external command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is explicit and confirmed in context. Risk is moderated because the executable is fixed and shell execution is not used.

medium_findings.title: Network Access Through GitHub Discovery
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill instructs agents to use web_search or gh api, and the helper queries GitHub repository search through gh api. This is expected behavior, but it sends search activity to GitHub or a search provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflow and script both show external GitHub lookup behavior. It appears functional rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited lines contain descriptive text, a GitHub search query, and report field labels. No evidence found of cryptographic hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines were manually checked and do not contain cryptographic operations. The static matches appear to be keyword or pattern artifacts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for most cited SKILL.md lines. They are markdown inline code labels or a documentation example, not Ruby backtick execution. The example command still supports the external-command risk already covered.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown content, not executable Ruby code. One shell command example exists, but it is documentation rather than automatic execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script invokes the GitHub CLI with subprocess.run. This should be allowed only with user awareness of local command execution and GitHub authentication context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present. The fixed executable and argument-list invocation reduce but do not remove operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External GitHub Queries
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow and helper script perform repository discovery through GitHub search or web search. This creates expected network access and external service dependency.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both documentation and script behavior support the network finding. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or covert endpoints.

content.value_statement: Finding useful open source issues takes time because repositories, labels, and maintainer activity must be compared. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code search GitHub and produce a practical contribution dossier.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to discover active GitHub repositories by stars, recent activity, and topic relevance.
Targets issue labels such as help wanted, good first issue, bug, v1, and roadmap.
Uses the GitHub CLI helper to collect repository and issue candidates when gh is available.
Evaluates issues for reproducibility, user impact, maintainer activity, and implementation complexity.
Produces contribution dossiers with project context, issue links, proposed strategy, and confidence scoring.
limitations: Requires GitHub access through web search or the gh CLI for best results.
Results depend on repository metadata, labels, and issue quality.
Large repositories may exceed the available context for deep code inspection.
The skill cannot guarantee that maintainers will accept a pull request.

use_cases.title: Find First Contributions
use_cases.description: Identify approachable issues with clear labels and enough context for a first pull request.

use_cases.title: Prioritize High-Impact Fixes
use_cases.description: Compare bug reports across active repositories and select issues likely to help many users.

use_cases.title: Build Contribution Plans
use_cases.description: Prepare a short dossier with issue context, likely root cause, and an initial fix strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Find Beginner Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Find open source issues in active JavaScript repositories that are suitable for a first contribution. Prefer clear help wanted or good first issue labels.

prompt_templates.title: Search a Specific Repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Hunt for bug fix opportunities in owner/repo. Summarize each issue, why it looks mergeable, and what first step I should take.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Domains
prompt_templates.prompt: Find promising help wanted issues in AI tooling and developer productivity repositories. Compare impact, complexity, and maintainer responsiveness.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Contribution Dossier
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a contribution dossier for the best three current issues you find. Include project context, issue links, risk, fix strategy, and confidence score.

output_examples.input: Find me good first issues in active AI repositories.
output_examples.output: A ranked shortlist with repository names, issue links, labels, project activity, why each issue is approachable, and suggested first actions.

output_examples.input: Hunt for bugs in langchain-ai/langchain that could become quick pull requests.
output_examples.output: A concise dossier for selected bugs, including likely reproduction path, affected area, implementation complexity, and mergeability notes.

output_examples.input: Compare contribution opportunities in Web3 tooling repositories.
output_examples.output: A comparison table in prose form that weighs issue clarity, user impact, maintainer responsiveness, and confidence for each candidate.

best_practices: Review issue comments before starting work to avoid duplicate pull requests.
Confirm that the repository accepts community contributions before investing time.
Ask the agent to explain uncertainty when issue details or maintainer activity are sparse.
anti_patterns: Do not treat labels alone as proof that an issue is easy or high impact.
Do not run GitHub CLI commands without understanding the account and authentication context.
Do not submit generated fixes without testing and reading project contribution guidelines.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically submit pull requests?
faq.answer: No. It helps find and analyze issues, but users must review, implement, test, and submit changes.

faq.question: Does it require the GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: The included helper uses gh. The documented workflow can also use web search when available.

faq.question: Can it inspect any repository?
faq.answer: It can analyze public repository information that the agent can access. Private repositories require proper user access.

faq.question: How does it rank opportunities?
faq.answer: It considers issue clarity, impact, maintainer activity, complexity, and whether the work appears achievable.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace use?
faq.answer: It has medium operational risk because it can use network access and local GitHub CLI commands. No confirmed malicious behavior was found.

faq.question: Can it guarantee pull request acceptance?
faq.answer: No. Maintainer priorities, project policies, and review quality determine whether a pull request is accepted.
